     The Numbers Business Report

Let Him Go (1970)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $9,358,025Details
International Box Office $3,227,174
Worldwide Box Office $12,585,199
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $2,472,847
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $2,002,884
Total $4,475,731
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Following the loss of their son, retired sheriff George Blackledge and his wife Margaret leave their Montana ranch to rescue their young grandson from the clutches of a dangerous family living off the grid in the Dakotas, headed by matriarch Blanche Weboy. When they discover the Weboys have no intention of letting the child go, George and Margaret are left with no choice but to fight for their family.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$4,000,470 (42.7% of total gross)
Legs:2.34 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:74.4% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Theater counts:2,454 opening theaters/2,458 max. theaters, 4.3 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $12,190,660

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: November 6th, 2020 (Wide) by Focus Features
International Releases: January 1st, 1970 (Wide) (Belgium)
November 4th, 2020 (Wide) (Trinidad)
November 5th, 2020 (Wide) (South Korea)
November 5th, 2020 (Wide) (United Arab Emirates)
November 5th, 2020 (Wide) (Saudi Arabia)
Video Release: November 24th, 2020 by Universal Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: R for violence.
(Rating bulletin 2642 (Cert #52208), 8/12/2020)
Running Time: 113 minutes
Plot point: Death of a Son or Daughter, Rescue
Subgenre: Crime Thriller
Time period setting: 1960s
Source:Based on Fiction Book/Short Story
Genre:Thriller/Suspense
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Mazur/Kaplan Company, Focus Features
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

Following the loss of their son, retired sheriff George Blackledge and his wife Margaret leave their Montana ranch to rescue their young grandson from the clutches of a dangerous family living off the grid in the Dakotas, headed by matriarch Blanche Weboy. When they discover the Weboys have no intention of letting the child go, George and Margaret are left with no choice but to fight for their family.
